content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

December 10, 1990

Olive Wehbring  
4219 Governor Drive  
San Diego, CA 92122

RE: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. G-90-696

Dear Ms. Wehbring:

This is in response to your November 2 and November 24, 1990, requests, on behalf of the League of Women Voters of San Diego, to find out whether the commissioners of the San Diego Unified Port District are subject to the Political Reform Act (the "Act," copy enclosed).1 This is also a follow up to our November 27 phone conversation, during which you informed me that the district's authority is limited to San Diego County and that your request is aimed at the conflict of interest provisions of the Act.

As you and I discussed over the phone, we normally provide only general information in response to requests made by persons who are not the subject of the inquiry. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B),(c)(4)(C).) We can tell you, however, that local government agencies (defined in Section 82041) are covered by conflict of interest provisions in the Act, most notably Sections 84308, 87100 and 87103. (Also enclosed is a copy of our pamphlet on conflict of interest provisions in the Act.)

Additionally, the Act requires local government agencies to adopt their own conflict of interest codes. (Section 87300.) Regulation 18750(m) (copy enclosed) pertains to local agencies whose jurisdiction is limited to one county, and it requires the chief executive officer of each such agency to maintain copies of the agency's conflict of interest code. It also requires agencies to make their conflict of interest codes available to the public.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

The Act defines "local government agency" broadly to mean a "county, city or district of any kind including...any department, bureau, office, board, commission, or other agency of the foregoing." We have advised another port district (the port district of Santa Cruz) that it is a local agency under this definition. (Advice Letter, No. 77-225, copy enclosed.) However, the determination that an entity is a local government agency for purposes of the Act must be made in a case-by-case basis.

I hope this has been of some help to you. If you need any additional information, please feel free to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

Steven R. Rhoads
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:SR:dg
Enclosures
